name: Here4Solar UK solar export tariff and SEG licensee coverage dataset
description: A dated editorial dataset covering every supplier on Ofgem's 2026/27 SEG licensee list plus Good Energy's public export tariffs. It records verified flat rates and explicitly marks official sites where no public rate was located.
coverage: All 16 suppliers on Ofgem's 2026/27 SEG licensee list, plus Good Energy public export products for market context.
methodology: Check Ofgem's current 2026/27 SEG supplier list for licensee status.
Check the supplier's own public site for a current flat export rate and visible eligibility conditions.
Exclude dynamic and time-of-use tariffs from the single numeric rate field.
Use not_stated rather than infer a condition absent from the public summary.
Add an explicit no-public-rate-located record rather than copy an unverified secondary-source rate.
Retain Good Energy's public export products for market context while marking that the company is not on Ofgem's current SEG licensee list.

columns.legal_licensee: Legal supplier entity, or the public export provider for a non-list market-context row.
columns.tariff: Public tariff name, or a transparent status label if no public rate was located.
columns.rate_p_per_kwh: Advertised flat export rate in pence per kWh; null when no official public rate was located.
columns.rate_type: How the official page describes the price or term, including unresolved wording conflicts.
columns.import_supplier_required: Whether the public summary explicitly requires the same company to supply imported electricity: yes, no or not_stated.
columns.installer_or_purchase_required: Whether the public summary explicitly ties the rate to a qualifying installation or purchase route: yes, no or not_stated.
columns.battery_required: Whether a battery is explicitly required for this rate: yes, no or not_stated.
columns.system_size_public_summary: Capacity condition shown in the public summary where relevant.
columns.checked_date: Date the source page was checked, ISO 8601.
columns.source_url: Primary supplier source checked for the record.
columns.notes: Important eligibility, ambiguity or verification note. The supplier's current contract governs.

rows.system_size_public_summary: not stated on public tariff summary
rows.notes: For customers whose solar panels and battery were installed by Good Energy Solar; rolls to Solar Savings after the fixed term.

rows.tariff: SEG Install Exclusive - solar and battery
rows.system_size_public_summary: under 30kW
rows.notes: OVO must install the solar panels and home-storage battery; other published criteria also apply.

rows.tariff: So Solar Smart Export Guarantee - first year
rows.rate_type: published first-year rate; current tariff letter governs
rows.system_size_public_summary: public solar-installation pages direct readers to current eligibility and tariff terms
rows.notes: So Energy advertises 20p/kWh with its solar proposition; its supporting article says this is the first-year rate and describes 4.5p/kWh after that. The public summary does not clearly state every import-supply condition.

rows.rate_type: 12-month offer; term sheet governs price status
rows.system_size_public_summary: 50kW or less
rows.notes: Qualifying EDF or Contact Solar route for solar, battery or both after 2 March 2026; see the tariff terms and term sheet.

rows.system_size_public_summary: residential renewable generation system up to 15kW
rows.notes: For a solar and/or battery installation by E.ON Installation Services from 10 November 2025; the public summary does not state an import-supply requirement.

rows.system_size_public_summary: solar PV; public page directs readers to eligibility criteria
rows.notes: Available to existing residential EDF electricity customers, subject to published eligibility and terms.

rows.system_size_public_summary: under 30kW
rows.notes: OVO must install the solar panels; other published criteria also apply.

rows.system_size_public_summary: eligible SEG generation; tariff-specific terms apply
rows.notes: Requires ScottishPower import supply and solar panels and/or a solar battery installed by ScottishPower.

rows.system_size_public_summary: residential renewable generation system up to 15kW
rows.notes: Requires an E.ON Next import tariff, excluding the time-of-use tariffs identified by E.ON Next.

rows.system_size_public_summary: systems up to 15kW
rows.notes: British Gas must supply the property's electricity; exclusions in the full terms apply.

rows.system_size_public_summary: not stated on public tariff summary
rows.notes: Available to Good Energy electricity-supply customers with solar panels; the variable rate can move up or down.

rows.system_size_public_summary: eligible renewable generation and export metering required
rows.notes: Requires an Octopus import account and a compatible import tariff; rates can change with notice.

rows.system_size_public_summary: under 30kW
rows.notes: For OVO electricity-supply customers; the installation does not have to have been supplied by OVO.

rows.system_size_public_summary: eligible SEG generation; tariff-specific terms apply
rows.notes: Requires ScottishPower import electricity at the same address as the installation.

rows.system_size_public_summary: systems over 15kW
rows.notes: British Gas must supply the property's electricity; exclusions in the full terms apply.

rows.system_size_public_summary: eligible SEG generation; public tariff terms apply
rows.notes: Requires Utility Warehouse Energy plus two or more additional qualifying services such as broadband, mobile or insurance.

rows.system_size_public_summary: renewable generation system up to 5MW
rows.notes: The public eligibility summary describes new and existing customers but does not state an import-supply requirement.

rows.system_size_public_summary: eligible SEG generation; tariff-specific terms apply
rows.notes: The public tariff summary does not state an import-supply condition for the standard SmartGen route.

rows.system_size_public_summary: eligible generation up to 5MW; tariff-specific limits can apply
rows.notes: Exclusive to EDF electricity customers; EDF says the rate can move up or down.

rows.system_size_public_summary: up to 5MW, or 50kW for micro-CHP
rows.notes: Pozitive publishes 5p/kWh for all eligible exported energy and says payments are made twice yearly. No import-supply requirement is stated on the public summary.

rows.tariff: So Energy standard export rate after first year
rows.rate_type: published follow-on rate; commencement letter governs
rows.system_size_public_summary: public article describes the rate after the first year of a So Energy solar route
rows.notes: So Energy's official article says it pays 20p/kWh in the first year of solar and 4.5p/kWh after that. Its general SEG page says non-supply customers can apply, but the actual rate and conditions are set in the Smart Export Commencement Letter.

rows.system_size_public_summary: eligible renewable generation and export metering required
rows.notes: Octopus describes this as an export-only route that can be combined with another supplier's import tariff.

rows.system_size_public_summary: up to 5MW, or 50kW for micro-CHP
rows.notes: OVO says the home's electricity supply can be with any provider.

rows.system_size_public_summary: up to 5MW, or 50kW for micro-CHP
rows.notes: Voltx publishes 3.02p/kWh for eligible exported energy and twice-yearly payments. No import-supply requirement is stated on the public summary.

rows.system_size_public_summary: public page describes the route for non-British-Gas customers and other specified cases
rows.notes: British Gas says this rate applies if it does not supply the property's electricity, and to certain other applicant categories.

rows.system_size_public_summary: up to 5MW, or 50kW for micro-CHP
rows.notes: E publishes one variable SEG tariff paying 3p/kWh. No import-supply requirement is stated in the public eligibility summary.

rows.system_size_public_summary: eligible generation up to 5MW; tariff-specific limits can apply
rows.notes: Available whether EDF or another supplier provides the home's imported electricity.

rows.rate_type: official page uses both variable and fixed wording; contract governs
rows.system_size_public_summary: up to 5MW, or 50kW for micro-CHP
rows.notes: Ruby publishes 3p/kWh and says no import-tariff switch is required. Its page describes the offer as both variable-rate and fixed-rate, so the signed terms should resolve that inconsistency.

rows.system_size_public_summary: up to 5MW, or 50kW for micro-CHP
rows.notes: Utilita publishes 3p/kWh and states that applicants do not need to be Utilita import customers.

rows.system_size_public_summary: eligible SEG generation; public tariff terms apply
rows.notes: Utility Warehouse describes the standard 2p/kWh tariff as available to everyone who meets the SEG requirements.

rows.tariff: Public rate not located on official site
rows.system_size_public_summary: not stated with a public rate
rows.notes: Ofgem lists Fuse Energy Supply Limited as a mandatory 2026/27 SEG licensee. Fuse publishes SEG explainers, but Here4Solar did not locate a public official tariff rate in those pages on the checked date, so no secondary-source rate is inserted.

rows.tariff: Public rate not located on official site
rows.system_size_public_summary: not stated with a public rate
rows.notes: Ofgem lists Foxglove Energy Supply Limited as a mandatory 2026/27 SEG licensee. Here4Solar did not locate a public SEG rate or application page on the official Outfox site on the checked date, so no secondary-source rate is inserted.

rows.tariff: Public rate not located on official site
rows.system_size_public_summary: not stated with a public rate
rows.notes: Ofgem lists Smart Pay Energy Ltd as a voluntary 2026/27 SEG licensee. Here4Solar did not locate a public SEG tariff or rate on the official site on the checked date.
